title: Customers With Early Peak Spending
description: Find customers whose highest order amount appears within the first floor(total_orders / 2) orders after sorting each customer's orders by date.
sql_solution: WITH ranked AS (SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY date) rn, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id) total FROM orders) SELECT customer_id FROM ranked GROUP BY customer_id HAVING MAX(CASE WHEN rn <= total/2 THEN amount END) = MAX(amount);
pyspark_solution:
from pyspark.sql.functions import *
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

ranking_window = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(to_date(col("date")))
count_window = Window.partitionBy("customer_id")

ranked_orders = (
    orders.withColumn("rn", row_number().over(ranking_window))
    .withColumn("total", count(lit(1)).over(count_window))
)

result = (
    ranked_orders.groupBy("customer_id")
    .agg(
        max(
            when(
                col("rn") <= floor(col("total") / lit(2)).cast("int"),
                col("amount")
            )
        ).alias("first_half_peak"),
        max("amount").alias("max_amount")
    )
    .filter(col("first_half_peak") == col("max_amount"))
    .select("customer_id")
)
